intro: Exitelia corymbosa, also known as East Indian spikenard, is a climbing vine native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions, due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Exitelia corymbosa include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. In herbal medicine, Exitelia corymbosa is commonly prepared as an extract or tincture, often combined with other herbs to create formulations for stress relief, sleep support, and skin health.
